name: Rosa Morales

handling.tone: Warmest active conversation. She explicitly asked for email details. Andre responded today (Apr 25) with details + 2 qualifying questions. Wait for response, don't pile on.
handling.voice_adjustments: Andre voice: warm, listening register. 21st birthday = emotional milestone for the family. Walk in as advisor not vendor.
handling.context: Inbound Apr 24 from FB Lead Form (~7:15 PM Friday). Asked specifically for emailed details. Andre responded Apr 25 with framework + asked guest count confirmation + service style. 6 weeks to event = HIGH urgency. MSN.com email suggests older planner (mom-as-planner scenario likely).

context_anchors: 21st Birthday June 6, 2026 — 6 weeks out, HIGH urgency
30 guests, $1,040 ballpark = appetizer-heavy or drop-off + light service
MSN.com email + Friday-evening form = older demo, mom-planner skew
Asked specifically for email details — text-light
Morales = common Latin surname, cuisine direction open question

lead.source: FB Lead Form — 01. [CATER] LF EVERGREEN - QUOTE
lead.last_activity: 2026-04-25 — Andre sent catering details email with 2 qualifying questions
lead.next_move: Wait 24-48h for reply; if silent 72h, one warm SMS nudge ('did the email come through?')

enrichment.identity_notes: 'Rosa Morales' one of most common Latin-American names in MA. 617 area = Boston metro. MSN email + Friday-evening form pattern slightly skews older demographic (mom-as-planner) over Gen-Z self-planner. Two scenarios: (1) mom planning for someone turning 21, or (2) younger Rosa with low public footprint. Don't assume — open with 'whose 21st?'
win_angles: Emotional milestone weight — 21st birthdays are rite-of-passage
She's already leaning in — requested details, will reply in 24-48h
Right-size the package — 30 + $1,040 = appetizer-heavy or drop-off + light service
Cultural door open — Morales = Latin surname, ask cuisine direction
Win the relationship — if mom-planner, more events come (quinceañeras, graduations)
nepq_openers: After her reply: 'Rosa, thanks for getting back. Quick — are you the planner here or the birthday person? That changes how I think about the night for you. Also, cuisine: Latin/family-style food, more American party menu, or open?'
Frame (mom): 'For a mom putting together her daughter's 21st, what are you most worried about getting wrong?'
Anchor: 'For 30 people, drop-off / light service / fully hosted = $X-Y. Want a tight comparison so you can pick what feels right?'
open_gaps: Cuisine direction (Latin vs American vs mixed)
enrichment.strategic_note: Warmest thing in active set. Came in Friday, asked for emailed details, Andre responded today with details + 2 qualifying questions. Now wait. Job here: listen, mirror, show Comeketo gets the moment. Walk in as advisor not vendor. Patience and one well-crafted follow-up if she goes silent.
